title: Contact page renders duplicate canonical and hreflang links under E2E production-start environment

evidence.reference: CI=1 pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/seo-validation.spec.ts --project=chromium --grep "Contact"
evidence.summary: Reproduced 2 failed SEO tests: canonical and hreflang locators resolve to both localhost and production URL elements on /en/contact.

evidence.reference: manual Playwright DOM probe against http://localhost:3000/en/contact with E2E-like pnpm start env
evidence.summary: DOM contained canonical/hreflang set for http://localhost:3000/en/contact and a second set for https://tianze-pipe.com/en/contact.
Contact page uses path-aware generateMetadataForPath for canonical and hreflang metadata.
generateMetadataForPath rewrites alternates from SITE_CONFIG.baseUrl, so mixed base URL ownership can duplicate metadata when static and streamed metadata disagree.

impact: The main inquiry page can send contradictory SEO signals and fail the repo's own SEO E2E gate. For a buyer-conversion page, this is a launch blocker even though normal unit tests pass.
root_cause: Canonical base URL ownership is split between build-time/static prerender output and runtime E2E start environment. Next.js 16 can stream metadata for dynamic pages, so the Contact route can end up with a build-time localhost set and a runtime production set.
recommended_fix: Make Contact metadata use one canonical base URL source per environment. Rebuild and start with the same URL contract, or change the metadata helper/test env so static and streamed metadata cannot disagree. Do not relax the SEO test.
verification_needed: Rerun the targeted Contact SEO E2E, then the full seo-validation.spec.ts, then pnpm ci:local or the E2E stage that previously failed.

title: Public buyer-facing content still contains placeholder contact and product assets

evidence.summary: Public contact phone is still +86-518-0000-0000.
Brand asset paths are explicitly marked as intentional placeholders.
The shipped product image says Sample Product and asks to replace it with a real product photo.
Product spec image slots still point to /images/products/sample-product.svg; the same placeholder appears across 10 product-family slots.

impact: Overseas B2B buyers can see fake-looking phone numbers and sample product imagery. This directly weakens supplier trust and inquiry conversion.
root_cause: Owner-dependent launch assets were intentionally left as placeholders but are now present on public buyer-facing surfaces.
recommended_fix: Replace with verified owner-provided phone, logo, OG image, and real product/factory images; or hide image blocks until real assets exist.
verification_needed: Run a placeholder scan for phone/sample-product/logo TODOs, then visually smoke product and contact pages in both locales.

title: Next.js middleware convention is deprecated while repo Cloudflare rules still require middleware.ts

evidence.summary: Build passes but prints: The middleware file convention is deprecated. Please use proxy instead.
Cloudflare/OpenNext build passes but prints the same middleware deprecation warning.
Repo rule says middleware.ts is the Cloudflare-compatible entry and src/proxy.ts previously blocked pnpm build:cf.
Installed Next.js 16.2.4 docs state middleware is deprecated and proxy.ts is the renamed convention.

impact: The site builds today, but future Next/OpenNext upgrades may turn this warning into a harder migration cost. Changing it casually could also break Cloudflare.
root_cause: Framework convention changed faster than the repo-specific Cloudflare compatibility rule was revalidated.
recommended_fix: Create a dedicated Cloudflare proof lane for middleware.ts to proxy.ts migration, including pnpm build, pnpm build:cf, preview:cf, and smoke:cf:preview before changing the rule.
verification_needed: A branch that renames the entry must pass Next build, OpenNext build, Cloudflare local preview, and page smoke.

title: full-project-health-v2 project profile is stale

evidence.summary: Profile lists TypeScript 5.9, next-intl 4.8, and .next-docs/ as the docs path.
Current package uses Next.js 16.2.4, React 19.2.5, TypeScript ^6.0.3, Tailwind ^4.2.4, and next-intl ^4.11.0.
.next-docs does not exist; installed Next docs exist under node_modules/next/dist/docs.

impact: Future audit runs can start from stale stack assumptions or miss the correct version-locked Next.js documentation source.
root_cause: The project adapter was not updated after dependency and documentation-source changes.
recommended_fix: Update project-profile.md to match package.json and AGENTS.md: current versions, Node 24 baseline, and node_modules/next/dist/docs.
verification_needed: Rerun validate_audit_config.py and manually confirm the adapter's stack facts against package.json and installed docs.

title: review:mutation:critical package script points to a missing file

evidence.summary: review:mutation:critical is defined as node scripts/review-mutation-critical.js.
Command fails with MODULE_NOT_FOUND for scripts/review-mutation-critical.js.
Related review scripts exist, but scripts/review-mutation-critical.js is absent.

impact: Normal test and build gates still pass, but an advertised critical-path mutation review lane is unusable, so future reviewers can get false confidence or lose time.
root_cause: The package script survived after its implementation file was removed or never added.
recommended_fix: Either restore the intended script or delete/rename the package script to the real mutation guard command.
verification_needed: Run pnpm review:mutation:critical or its replacement and record the expected pass/fail contract.

title: Sensitive server-only files do not consistently include import "server-only"

evidence.summary: Repo security rule says sensitive server files should add import "server-only".
Only src/app/api/inquiry/route.ts currently has import "server-only" among the checked sensitive files.
Public write route starts with next/server imports, not import "server-only".
Lead processing module is server-sensitive but has no import "server-only" marker.

impact: This is not a confirmed exploit because API routes are server-only by framework boundary. The risk is future accidental client imports or unclear server/client ownership.
root_cause: The security convention is documented but not systematically applied across existing sensitive modules.
recommended_fix: Add import "server-only" to sensitive route/pipeline/store files where compatible, and keep tests/build as the regression guard.
verification_needed: Run type-check, lint, tests touching API/lead/security, and build after adding the markers.

title: Layout owns the global main landmark while page shells add nested main landmarks

evidence.summary: The localized root layout already wraps all page children in <main id="main-content">.
Current runtime source contains 11 <main> declarations outside tests, including page shells nested under the layout main.
Key buyer-facing pages render additional page-level main landmarks inside the layout-owned main wrapper.
Existing tests assert that a main landmark exists, but no current test asserts exactly one composed main landmark per page.

impact: Real pages can contain nested or multiple main landmarks. The skip-link still points to the outer layout wrapper, but page semantics become noisy for screen readers and accessibility tooling.
root_cause: The repo has two competing ownership models: the app layout owns the document main landmark, while individual pages and content shells also render their own main landmarks.
recommended_fix: Choose one owner. Given the current layout skip link, keep the layout <main id="main-content"> and convert page/shell <main> wrappers to div, section, or article containers where appropriate. Add a regression check for one main landmark on key pages.
verification_needed: Run the no-JS HTML contract and navigation/accessibility smoke tests, then verify key pages have exactly one main landmark after full layout composition.

title: Production config validation does not catch buyer-facing placeholder assets

evidence.summary: The command passes with 'Production configuration validated successfully' while public placeholder phone and product assets still exist.
isPlaceholder only matches bracket-style values such as [PLACEHOLDER_NAME].
getUnconfiguredPlaceholders checks a narrow set of SITE_CONFIG fields and does not inspect placeholder-like phone numbers, public image paths, copied message bundles, or Terms content.

evidence.reference: rg -n "\\+86-518-0000-0000|Sample Product|Replace this image|/images/products/sample-product\\.svg" src content messages public
evidence.summary: Static scans still find the fake phone and sample-product asset across public config, messages, Terms content, product specs, and public SVG assets.

impact: A command named production configuration validation can be green while the exact buyer-trust placeholders that block launch remain public. Future release checks can therefore give a false sense of readiness.
root_cause: The validation contract is too narrow: it validates runtime/env and bracket placeholders, but not public-launch trust placeholders that look like realistic template data.
recommended_fix: Either rename the command to reflect its narrow runtime scope or extend it with a public-launch placeholder guard for fake phone patterns, sample product assets, placeholder SVG text, Terms/contact copies, and brand asset TODOs. If no real phone exists, the passing state should be intentionally hidden, not fake data allowed.
verification_needed: Rerun pnpm validate:config and the placeholder scan after repair; the validator should fail on current placeholders and pass only when public placeholders are replaced or intentionally suppressed.
